title: Lesson Quiz: Digital Access, Inclusion & Participation
description: Assessment of key concepts aligned to the lesson objective.

show_feedback.prompt: What does the term "digital divide" refer to?

choices.text: Differences in access to technology and the internet
choices.feedback: Correct. The digital divide refers to gaps in access to digital resources.
The digital divide is about access, not device types.
Communication styles are not related to access gaps.
The digital divide focuses on access, not rules.

show_feedback.prompt: Which example best demonstrates digital inclusion?

choices.feedback: Inclusion removes barriers, not creates them.

choices.text: Designing technology that is accessible to all users, including those with disabilities
choices.feedback: Correct. Inclusion ensures everyone can participate in digital environments.

choices.text: Requiring advanced skills to use basic tools
choices.feedback: Inclusion simplifies access, not complicates it.

show_feedback.prompt: Digital participation means actively engaging and contributing in online environments.

choices.feedback: Correct. Participation involves active engagement, communication, and contribution.
Participation is about active involvement, not passive use.

choices.text: It ensures only advanced users can use technology
choices.feedback: Digital access is about inclusion, not exclusivity.

choices.text: It allows individuals to participate, learn, and communicate online
choices.feedback: Correct. Access enables participation in digital environments.

choices.text: It limits how people use technology
choices.feedback: Access expands opportunities, not limits them.

choices.text: It replaces the need for communication
choices.feedback: Technology supports communication, it does not replace it.

show_feedback.prompt: Which action best supports digital equity?

choices.text: Providing equal access to devices and internet resources
choices.feedback: Correct. Digital equity ensures fair access for all users.

choices.text: Allowing access only to experienced users
choices.feedback: Equity supports all users, not just experienced ones.
Addressing gaps is essential for equity.
Equity is about access, not device quality.
